channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Andrew Ng's Vision for AI: From Brain-Inspired Models to Agentic Workflows
item.description: Andrew Ng's 15-year-old theory on brain-inspired machines and agentic workflows is gaining traction, with Google, Microsoft, and Anthropic embracing the concept.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Arm’s high-stakes licensing suit against Qualcomm ends in mistrial, but Qualcomm prevails in key areas
item.description: Arm has lost a battle over licensing of its microprocessor designs to Qualcomm, ending doubt over the immediate future of some of the chip maker’s products.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Mastering Azure management: A comparative analysis of leading cloud platforms
item.description: Azure’s growing adoption among companies leveraging cloud platforms highlights the increasing need for effective cloud resource management.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Amazon and Starbucks workers are on strike. Trump might have something to do with it
item.description: Amazon delivery drivers and Starbucks baristas are on strike in a handful of U.S. cities as they seek to exert pressure on the two major companies to recognize them as unionized employees or to meet demands for an inaugural labor contract.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Democrat accuses Musk of tanking spending bill over China restrictions
item.description: The top Democrat on the House Appropriations Committee accused Elon Musk of tanking a bipartisan spending bill because it included a provision that could limit his businesses' ability to operate in China. In a Friday letter to congressional leaders, Rep. Rosa DeLauro (D-Conn.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, The Hill

item.title: Amazon workers are striking at multiple facilities. Here's what you should know
item.description: Amazon workers affiliated with the Teamsters union continued a strike Friday at seven of the company's delivery hubs just days before Christmas. At midnight on Saturday, workers at a prominent unionized warehouse in New York will also join, the Teamsters said.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: More CISA staff would keep working under DHS’ revised shutdown plan
item.description: Under a new plan, about one-third of CISA's staff will keep working through a shutdown. But experts are still concerned about the impact on U.S. cyber defenses. The post More CISA staff would keep working under DHS’ revised shutdown plan first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Scholz dismisses Musk's assertion that only a far-right party can 'save' Germany
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Chancellor Olaf Scholz on Friday dismissed an assertion by Elon Musk that only a far-right party can “save Germany,” but said that freedom of opinion "also goes for multibillionaires.” Germany is expected to vote in an early election on Feb.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Norton Revamps VPN Offering with Kill Switch Feature
item.description: Norton, the popular antivirus solution, has revamped its VPN offering by adding a crucial kill switch feature, making it more secure for Mac users.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: How to perform a clean install in Windows 10 and 11
item.description: One key technique in the Windows repair playbook involves wiping out everything on the storage device (typically C:\) from which Windows boots and on which that OS resides. Prosaically enough, this device is often called the boot/system drive or disk.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What is data architecture? A framework to manage data
item.description: Data architecture definition Data architecture describes the structure of an organization’s logical and physical data assets, and data management resources, according to The Open Group Architecture Framework (TOGAF).
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Revolutionizing policing: Kazakhstan’s trailblazing Digital Policeman initiative
item.description: In the era of digital transformation, public safety stands at a critical crossroads. Law enforcement agencies globally are under increasing scrutiny to enhance transparency, efficiency, and trust within their communities.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Smart policing revolution: How Kazakhstan is setting a global benchmark
item.description: In the era of digital transformation, public safety stands at a critical crossroads. Law enforcement agencies globally are under increasing scrutiny to enhance transparency, efficiency, and trust within their communities.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Amazon workers strike at multiple facilities as Teamsters seek labor contract
item.description: Workers at seven Amazon facilities went on strike Thursday, an effort by the Teamsters union to pressure the e-commerce company for a labor agreement during a key shopping period.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Giant sloths and mastodons lived with humans for millennia in the Americas, new discoveries suggest
item.description: New discoveries from several archaeological sites in North and South America suggest that ancient people first arrived in the New World much earlier than scientists once thought.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DCSA details new plan for next-gen background investigation system
item.description: DCSA's new plan for NBIS includes a 36-month product roadmap. The goal is to retire legacy background investigation systems, including PIPs, by fiscal 2027. The post DCSA details new plan for next-gen background investigation system first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: New app reveals political affiliations to US homebuyers
item.description: The platform believes social factors like age, income, and education influence buyers' decisions over home layouts.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: Australian who falsely claimed to have invented bitcoin is found in contempt of UK court
item.description: LONDON (AP) — An Australian computer scientist who falsely claimed to be the founder of the bitcoin cryptocurrency was found Thursday to be in contempt of an order of London's High Court and was sentenced to 12 months in prison, suspended for two years.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Elon Musk considers funding Nigel Farage's populist party, a move that could shake up UK politics
item.description: LONDON (AP) — It’s a photo that sent a tremor through British politics: Elon Musk flanked by British politician Nigel Farage and a wealthy backer, in front of a gilt-framed painting of a young Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Massive data breach at federal credit union exposes 240,000 members
item.description: A credit union with over 240,000 members recently revealed it was targeted by cybercriminals, resulting in a data breach that was part of a two-month attack by hackers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple complains Meta requests risk privacy in spat over EU efforts to widen access to iPhone tech
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple complained that requests from Meta Platforms for access to its operating software threaten user privacy, in a spat fueled by the European Union's intensifying efforts to get the iPhone maker to open up to products from tech rivals.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: These 5 clever mapping upstarts do what Google Maps can’t
item.description: Look, Google Maps is fine. It’s arguably even great—probably one of the most-opened apps on lots of our phones and absolutely one of the most indispensable.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: FAQ: Getting started with Bluesky
item.description: What is Bluesky? Bluesky is a “microblogging” social media network similar to X (formerly Twitter), where people can publish posts with a maximum of 300 characters, as well as follow others, create lists, like posts, and so on.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: EPA fosters IT resilience through cloud, integrated teams, automation tools
item.description: EPA's CIO says modernized tools and integrated teams help the agency meet the changing needs of the public more easily and consistently. The post EPA fosters IT resilience through cloud, integrated teams, automation tools first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: EU investigates TikTok over Romanian presidential election safeguards
item.description: LONDON (AP) — European Union regulators said Tuesday they're investigating whether TikTok breached the bloc's digital rulebook by failing to deal with risks to Romania's presidential election, which has been thrown into turmoil over allegations of electoral violations and Russian meddling.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Smarter devops: How to avoid deployment horrors
item.description: Many devops organizations implement advanced CI/CD, infrastructure as code, and other automations to increase deployment frequency. In the State of DevOps Report 2023, 18% of respondents were classified as elite performers for being able to deploy on-demand with change lead times of less than a day.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to contact the Rhode Island cyberattack call center after data breach
item.description: Rhode Island officials urged residents to take immediate steps to protect themselves on Saturday, warning that the hackers could release personal data.
item.dc:creator: Leah Crowley, NewsNation

item.title: Senate report alleges Amazon rejected warehouse safety recommendations due to productivity concerns
item.description: At least two internal Amazon studies found a link between how quickly the online retailer's warehouse workers perform tasks and workplace injuries, but the company rejected many safety recommendations out of concern the proposed changes might reduce productivity, according to a U.S.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: CompTIA introduces foundational tech certification
item.description: CompTIA has created a certification designed to provide individuals with broad foundational and digital skills that can be used to build a career in technology.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Schumer calls for Mayorkas to ‘deploy special drone-detection tech’ in New York, New Jersey
item.description: “I’m pushing for answers amid these drone sightings,” Schumer posted on X. “I’m calling for @SecMayorkas to deploy special drone-detection tech across NY and NJ.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers tell Apple, Google to prepare for TikTok ban
item.description: TikTok now plans to appeal to the Supreme Court and has asked the D.C. Circuit to put the law on hold while it appeals the case.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation
